file_name: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT .txt

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, PPI hereby appoints EKR and EKR agrees to be retained as the exclusive distributor, and Authorized Distributor of Record, of the Products in the Field in the Territory during the Term to market, distribute, warehouse and sell the Products. EKR shall have the right to appoint sub-distributors hereunder in each country of the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms of this Agreement, PPI hereby appoints EKR and EKR agrees to be retained as the exclusive **vendor**, and Authorized Distributor of Record, of the Products in the Field in the Territory during the Term to market, distribute, warehouse and sell the Products. EKR shall have the right to appoint sub-distributors hereunder in each country of the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'distributor' is changed to 'vendor'. This introduces uncertainty because the agreement consistently refers to EKR as a 'distributor', defining their distribution rights and responsibilities. By introducing 'vendor' without defining it or clarifying its relationship to 'distributor', it creates ambiguity in EKR's role, specifically in Section 10.1, which assigns liabilities based on different roles.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PPI shall be liable for and shall defend, indemnify and hold harmless EKR and its Affiliates and their officers, directors, agents, representatives, consultants and employees (individually an "EKR Indemnified Party" and collectively the "EKR Indemnified Parties") and any of them from and against any and all Claims (as defined below), arising in connection with or relating to: (a) The development, manufacture, sale and supply of the Product prior to the Effective Date (including Claims arising after the Effective Date to the extent they are based on events occurring prior to the Effective Date); -44- (b) The manufacture of the Product by or on behalf of PPI (including, but not limited to, any manufacture of Product or any other product by EKR for the Other PPI Customers pursuant to Section 3.20(l)) except to the extent that such Claims arise from (i) the negligence or willful misconduct of EKR or its Affiliates, (ii) the breach by EKR of the terms of this Agreement or (iii) the manufacture of Product by EKR in accordance with EKR's exercise Step-in Right for supply of Product to EKR or its Affiliates; (c) Claims which arise outside the Territory (except to the extent that the Claim has arisen from any act or omission by EKR); (d) A breach by PPI of any representation, warranty, covenant or agreement contained in this Agreement, the Supply Agreement or the Transition Services and Inventory Agreement; (e) PPI's failure to comply with any Applicable Law in connection with the performance of its obligations hereunder or under the Supply Agreement or the Transition Services and Inventory Agreement, or prior to the Effective Date; and (f) Any Claims related to Product sold by parties other than EKR prior or subsequent to the Effective Date. (g) Liabilities arising under the ICS Agreement prior to the Effective Date and subsequent to the Effective Date for Products sold by parties other than EKR or under the direction of EKR or arising under the Transition Services and Inventory Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'distributor' to 'vendor' introduces ambiguity regarding EKR's role and associated liabilities. The contradicted text, Section 10.1, assigns liabilities based on EKR's role. The introduction of the term 'vendor' without clarification creates a conflict and uncertainty about which liabilities EKR assumes under this agreement. The agreement consistently refers to EKR as a distributor, defining their distribution rights and responsibilities. By introducing 'vendor' without defining it or clarifying its relationship to 'distributor', it creates ambiguity in EKR's role, specifically in Section 10.1. This meets the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: EKR shall, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR shall at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: EKR shall, as determined in its sole discretion to be commercially reasonable, prepare studies of the markets and sales potential of the Products for countries in the Territory other than the United States and present such studies to the Committee. EKR shall at its own cost and expense use commercially reasonable efforts to take those steps reasonably necessary in order to obtain and thereafter maintain Marketing Authorizations (including pricing and reimbursement approvals) for the Product in those countries of the Territory other than the United States which the Commercialization Committee determines to present commercially viable opportunities for the **Medication**.
perturbation.explanation: The term Product is changed to Medication. This introduces uncertainty because the agreement consistently refers to the "Product", defining their distribution rights and responsibilities. By introducing 'Medication' without defining it or clarifying its relationship to 'Product', it creates ambiguity in EKR's role, and can possibly create in-text contradiction in section 1.1 where Products is very well defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Product(s)" Means: (i) DepoDur Injectible Liposomal Epidural [**] mg/ml [**]; (ii) DepoDur Injectible Liposomal Epidural [**] mg/[**] ml [**]; (iii) such other presentations and dosages which hereafter receive Marketing Authorization in any country of the Territory; in each case for epidural administration presented in Vials or other approved vessels, appropriately packaged and labeled for sale to end users and (iv) any and all Improvements of the items listed in clauses (i) through (iii).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Product' to 'Medication' introduces a strong contradiction. The original text defines 'Product' specifically (e.g., DepoDur Injectible Liposomal Epidural [**] mg/ml [**]) and outlines EKR's responsibilities regarding it. By substituting 'Medication,' the modified text creates ambiguity about whether EKR's obligations now extend to something beyond the defined 'Product.' This directly conflicts with the clear definition of 'Product' in the contradicted text, making it unclear what EKR is responsible for concerning obtaining marketing authorizations.

perturbation.original_text: The indemnifying Party shall have the option to assume the defense thereof by election in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice. If the indemnifying Party fails to make such election, the indemnified Party may assume such defense and the indemnifying Party will be liable for reasonable legal and other expenses subsequently incurred in connection with such defense.
perturbation.changed_text: The indemnifying Party shall have the option to assume the defense thereof by election in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice. If the indemnifying Party fails to make such election, the indemnified Party may assume such defense and the indemnifying Party will be liable for reasonable legal and other expenses subsequently incurred in connection with such **litigation**.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'defense' with 'litigation' creates uncertainty as 'defense' refers to the broader process of defending against a claim, which includes pre-litigation activities. 'Litigation' is more narrowly focused on court proceedings. This could lead to disputes over whether the indemnifying party is liable for pre-litigation expenses. The section becomes contradictory because earlier it speaks of 'defense', making the scope of expenses unclear
perturbation.contradicted_text: Conditions to Indemnification. Promptly after receipt by a Party of any Claim or alleged claim or notice of the commencement of any action, administrative or legal proceeding, or investigation as to which the indemnity provided for in this Section 10 may apply, the indemnified Party shall give written notice to the indemnifying Party of such fact. The indemnifying Party shall have the option to assume the defense thereof by election in writing within thirty (30) days of receipt of such notice. If the indemnifying Party fails to make such election, the indemnified Party may assume such defense and the indemnifying Party will be liable for reasonable legal and other expenses subsequently incurred in connection with such litigation. The Parties will co-operate in good faith in the conduct of any defense, provide such reasonable assistance as may be required to enable any Claim to be properly defended, and the Party with conduct of the action shall provide promptly to the other Party copies of all proceedings relating to such action.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'defense' with 'litigation', which creates a strong in-text contradiction with the rest of the clause in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The original text and the contradicted text use 'defense' which encompasses pre-litigation activities, whilst the changed text uses 'litigation' which is more narrowly focused on court proceedings. Therefore, the change implies that the indemnifying party is only liable for expenses incurred during court proceedings, creating a direct conflict with the initial mention of 'defense', as the scope of expenses is narrowed. This creates uncertainty and thus a strong in-text contradiction.
